name: awesome-mobile-security

documentation.readme: > A practitioner-built reference for Android and iOS security — covering the full stack from app analysis and reverse engineering through kernel internals, malware research, and advanced platform exploitation. No fluff, no marketing copy. Built for security engineers who need to find the right tool or resource fast.
usage_sections: ZygiskFrida — Zygisk-based injection avoids frida-server process detection
Frida gadget mode — Embed frida-gadget.so directly in the app instead of running a server; harder to detect than an external frida-server process
Custom Frida builds — Rename Frida internals and recompile to evade string-based detection; community guides and forks available
objection's --startup-command — Inject bypass scripts at process initialization before detection logic runs
Shadow — Lightweight general-purpose jailbreak detection bypass. Low overhead, broad compatibility.

arguments.name: -aligned
arguments.description: insecure auth, data storage, network, IPC, code quality issues | `[Beginner–Intermediate]` |

arguments.name: -Analysis
arguments.description: **"A Systematic Analysis of Commercial Android Packers"** — ACM CCS 2022. Evaluates 12 commercial Android packer services, characterizing their protection mechanisms, detection eva

arguments.name: -Click
arguments.description: The BlastDoor Sandbox"** — Multiple speakers on the iMessage attack surface and the BlastDoor sandbox introduced in iOS 14 as a mitigation. Black Hat USA 2021. [Advanced]

arguments.name: -dive
arguments.description: what entitlements unlock, how the entitlement hierarchy is structured, and exploitation via entitlement abuse. OBTS v5.0. [Advanced]

arguments.name: -focused
arguments.description: iOS security architecture, vulnerability classes, and attack methodology. iOS 6-era (some chapters dated) but security architecture coverage remains foundational reading. `[Advance

arguments.name: -The-Wild
arguments.description: Excavating a Kernel Exploit"** — Seth Jenkins (Google). Analysis of kernel crash logs and artifacts from real-world Android exploit samples found in the wild. How to reconstruct ex

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --no-color
arguments.description: Disable ANSI color in output.

function_signatures.description: Index entry in the Mobile Security category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.

function_signatures.description: Pairs with the Mobile Security system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.

function_signatures.description: Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.

function_signatures.name: main
function_signatures.description: Top-level entry point for awesome-mobile-security (8kSec/awesome-mobile-security). Reads CLI args, dispatches to subcommands.

function_signatures.description: Top-level CLI runner for awesome-mobile-security. Returns a result envelope (ok, data, error).

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with vikramaadityam/SMPL_CS_PRJ_05_Exploiting_Android_with_Metasploit_Framwork (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with nowsecure/Exploiting-Android-WebViews-with-Frida (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with matheusado/Exploiting-Android-with-Metasploit (score=9); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['shell_android', 'shell_ios', 'web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['shell_android', 'shell_ios', 'web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['shell_android', 'shell_ios', 'web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_android, shell_ios, web] in the Mobile Security category. Fits when the engagement needs: Awesome Mobile Security.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: > A practitioner-built reference for Android and iOS security — covering the full stack from app analysis and reverse engineering through kernel internals, malware research, and advanced platform exploitation. No fluff, no marketing copy. Built for security engineers who need to find the right tool or resource fast.
documentation.how_to_use: Clone or install `awesome-mobile-security` from its upstream repository, then run `awesome-mobile-security --help` (or the README entrypoint) to confirm flags.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.
